steps.title: Rewrite both lines
steps.content: A system has no solution when the lines are parallel but distinct. Rewrite each equation in standard form: $$6x-5y=13y+4 \Rightarrow 6x-18y=4$$ $$ty=\frac{3}{2}+3x \Rightarrow -3x+ty=\frac{3}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the slopes
steps.content: Parallel lines have proportional $x$- and $y$-coefficients. Compare the second equation to the first: the $x$-coefficient changed from $6$ to $-3$, so the scale factor is $-\frac{1}{2}$. Apply that to the $y$-coefficient: $$-18\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=9$$ Thus $t=9$.

steps.title: Confirm no solution
steps.content: When $t=9$, the two lines have the same slope but different constants, so they are distinct parallel lines. They never intersect, so the system has no solution. Enter 9.
